texto: No system that implies control of society by privilege seekers has ever ended any other way but collapse
explicacao: Citação de William E. Dodd sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “No system that implies control of society by privilege seekers has ever ended any other way but c…” articula reflexao, politics, power em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e power.

texto: Governments from the top fail as often as those from the bottom; and every great failure brings a sad social reaction, thousands and millions of helpless men laying down their lives in the unhappy process. Why may not statesmen study the past and avoid such catastrophes?"Delivered in his capacity as the American Ambassador to Germany, on October 12, 1933, in a speech to the Berlin branch of the American Chamber of Commerce, quoted in Erik Larson's book, In the Garden of Beasts.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de William E. Dodd que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “Governments from the top fail as often as those from the bottom; and every great failure brings a…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre educacao, government, politics, com foco em estudos e politics.
